tool.name: spaCy NLP Annotators

param.name: input
param.label: Text
param.help: Plain text file to annotate. Its language must match the selected language model.

param.name: language_model
param.label: Language Model
param.help: Which installed spaCy model to use. Models are installed by an administrator via the spaCy Language Models data manager (Admin > Local Data); the model's language must match the input text.

param.name: annotators
param.label: Annotation types
param.help: Which spaCy pipeline components to include. Part of speech, NER, and dependency parsing each also run tokenization, POS, and lemmatization; NER and parsing are separate choices (neither includes the other).

param.name: format
param.label: Output format
param.help: JSON gives the full structured annotation. CoNLL and CoNLL-U are tabular Universal Dependencies formats; Text is a human-readable summary.

tool.help:
spaCy NLP =========
Galaxy wrapper for the `spaCy <https://spacy.io>`_ natural language processing library. spaCy provides industrial-strength NLP with support for 75+ languages.
Annotation Types ----------------
Tokenization and sentence segmentation Splits text into tokens and identifies sentence boundaries.
Part of speech and lemmas Includes tokenization plus part-of-speech (POS) tagging and lemmatization (reducing words to their base form).
Named entity recognition (NER) Includes tokenization, POS, and lemmas, plus identification of named entities such as PERSON, ORG, GPE, DATE, etc.
Dependency parsing Includes tokenization, POS, and lemmas, plus syntactic dependency parsing to identify grammatical relationships between words. (Named entity recognition is a separate selection and is not included.)
Output Formats --------------
**JSON** Comprehensive structured output with all annotations. Best for programmatic access.
**CoNLL** Tab-separated format suitable for dependency parsing tasks.
**CoNLL-U** Universal Dependencies format with morphological features.
**Text** Human-readable text output with statistics and annotations.
Language Models ---------------
spaCy uses trained pipeline models for each language. Models come in different sizes:
- **sm** (small): Fast, less accurate, ~10-20MB - **md** (medium): Balanced, includes word vectors, ~40-50MB - **lg** (large): Most accurate, large word vectors, ~500MB-800MB - **trf** (transformer): Transformer-based, highest accuracy, ~400MB-500MB
Install models using the data manager or manually with: ``python -m spacy download en_core_web_sm``
Supported Languages -------------------
spaCy supports 75+ languages at the tokenizer level, with trained statistical pipelines available from the data manager for 23 languages including:
- English (en) - Spanish (es) - German (de) - French (fr) - Chinese (zh) - Japanese (ja) - Portuguese (pt) - Italian (it) - Dutch (nl) - Greek (el) - And many more...
See https://spacy.io/usage/models for the complete list.
